query_normalized: the influence of algorithmic recommendations on exploration vs. exploitation in online learning psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on the technical optimization of recommender system architectures (AutoML, hardware co-design, and efficiency) rather than the psychological mechanisms of user behavior (exploration vs. exploitation) or the influence of recommendations on learning outcomes. It falls under the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule as it addresses engineering constraints in model design rather than the social-psychological constructs specified in the user's question.
